title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Prompt Forensics
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a coding-agent implementation brief and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Use a changed debug case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Change Scope trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.

content.text: For Change Scope, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Change Scope precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A total rewrite is implied whenever existing code is low quality.

stimulus.title: Change Scope case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A total rewrite is implied whenever existing code is low quality.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope. Artifact under review: a coding-agent implementation brief.

options.text: The Change Scope record says a coding-agent implementation brief looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Change Scope without its stated preconditions, omit the a coding-agent implementation brief trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Change Scope for a coding-agent implementation brief.
The record names the assumption, applies Change Scope, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-qneinl" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Implementation Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-implementation-evidence-qneinl
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Implementation Evidence structural check is only one gate. a coding-agent implementation brief must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Implementation Evidence precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Implementation Evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Implementation Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Implementation Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed debug case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Implementation Evidence through the concrete a coding-agent implementation brief case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
